name: Celery-Apple Salad with Currants
recipeInstructions: If the currants are hard, cover them with warm water and set them aside to soften while you cut the celery and apples. After about 10 minutes, drain the currants and squeeze out the water. Separate the stalks of celery and peel the tougher outer stalks. Slice the celery into thin pieces (straight across or at an angle). Cut the apples into quarters or sixths if they are large, then thinly slice them crosswise. Finely chop the celery leaves and parsley, and crack the walnuts. Combine the celery, apples, currants, celery leaves, parsley, and walnuts in a bowl. Toss with enough walnut oil to lightly coat everything. Add lemon juice or vinegar to taste, salt lightly, season with pepper, and toss again.
